scope.target: High-traffic document and config terminology normalization for active .NET source-of-truth clarity
scope.dependency_radius: Entry documents, script README, and shared config only
constraints: Preserve existing file paths and legacy script names where compatibility may matter
Clarify active-vs-historical meaning without rewriting the full documentation tree
Avoid bulk semantic cleanup outside this focused terminology slice
non_goals: Do not rename files or scripts in this stage
Do not rewrite every Java/Spring historical reference in the repository

inputs.architecture_target: Current-state documentation must clearly distinguish active .NET guidance from historical or migration-context references.
review_findings: High-traffic docs and script guidance still mix .NET and Java/Spring/JPA terminology.
project-config.yaml contains legacy-shaped fields that can be misread as active stack truth.
user_constraints: Continue under the documentation refactor workflow.

steps: Add active-vs-historical clarification to entry documents and script guidance.
Mark legacy-compatible config keys in project-config.yaml so maintainers do not misread them as active architectural truth.
Keep the scope limited to high-traffic sources that route later work.
validation: High-traffic docs now explicitly distinguish active .NET guidance from migration-context references.
No file rename or broad content migration was required for this stage.
Later stages can use these files without re-deciding whether Java/Spring terms are active.
deferred_items: AI asset and script naming redesign if compatibility allows.

results.summary: Clarified active .NET guidance versus legacy migration-context terminology in high-traffic docs and in project-config.yaml without renaming files or scripts.
